channel.title: Embedded-Security on CodetoCore
channel.description: Recent content in Embedded-Security on CodetoCore

item.title: Do I Need a Secure Element If My MCU Has a Crypto Accelerator?
item.description: Short answer: they solve different problems. A crypto accelerator makes crypto operations fast. A secure element makes sure your keys never leave the chip.

item.title: What TrustZone-M Adds to Your Build
item.description: Why moving from a plain Cortex-M to a TrustZone-M part doubles your project structure — and what each new piece is actually doing.

item.title: Hardware Root of Trust: What It Actually Means
item.description: Hardware Root of Trust is one of the most-used and least-understood terms in embedded security. A short, practical breakdown of what it is, what it isn't, and how to tell whether a product genuinely has one.

item.title: Why Your Secure Boot Probably Isn't Actually Secure
item.description: Most secure boot implementations look fine on paper but fail under realistic threat models. Eight common pitfalls — and what fixing them requires.
